caption: he ignores me for minecraft, so i built our whole relationship somewhere he'd actually look.

overlays.hook: he'd rather mine blocks than answer my texts, so i met him there.
beats: took the pickaxe from the spawn chest
our photos were waiting in the caverns
he can keep the diamonds. i want the vault.

rationale.summary: A first-person Blockheart Mine reel that turns 'he ignores me for minecraft' into the reason the gift lands. It plays the gamer-partner wedge for an anniversary audience and sends them to getforevermore.co, with an orientation beat so a cold viewer still learns what a Forevermore world is.
rationale.hook_reasoning: The open names a specific, true-feeling grievance every gamer's partner recognises, so the thumb stops before the payoff even arrives.
limits: kinetic-text video — no real Blockheart Mine footage exists yet, so the beats are on-screen text over motion, not a screen capture of the world.
rationale.audience: Partners of gamers shopping for an anniversary gift that meets him where he already is.

caption: i hid an anniversary letter behind a vault door he has to mine open himself. he's going to lose it.

overlays.hook: i turned our anniversary into a world he has to dig through to reach me.
beats: torch-lit signs point down into the caverns
every framed photo is one of us
six heart-gems set into the vault door
the golden room opens on my letter

rationale.summary: A build-anticipation Blockheart Mine reel framing the anniversary as a world he has to earn his way through. It runs the gamer-partner wedge for an anniversary audience and points to getforevermore.co, naming what the world is so a first-time viewer isn't lost.
rationale.hook_reasoning: Reframing 'anniversary' as a place he digs toward is a curiosity gap — the viewer wants to see what's behind the vault door.
limits: kinetic-text video — the vault reveal is described in overlays, not shown, because no captured Blockheart Mine footage is available yet.
rationale.audience: Partners of gamers who want an anniversary gift with a real payoff, not a card.

caption: pov: your girlfriend built you a whole minecraft world and the diamonds are just photos of you two.

overlays.hook: pov: she built you a world, and mining it out is the gift.
beats: chip away and a memory is framed in the rock
six hearts to earn, one vault to open
your letter's in the golden room

rationale.summary: A second-person POV Blockheart Mine reel addressed to the recipient, making the act of mining the world the gift itself. It plays the gamer-partner wedge for a him-focused gifting audience and links to getforevermore.co, spelling out what the world does so nobody's confused.
rationale.hook_reasoning: Second-person POV puts the viewer inside the gift; 'mining it out is the gift' reframes effort as delight and holds attention.
limits: kinetic-text video — the mechanic is narrated in overlays rather than shown, since real world footage for the Blockheart Mine isn't captured yet.
rationale.audience: People buying for a gamer partner who'd rather receive something they get to play through.
